basics.name: Taleef Tamsal
basics.label: Software Developer | AI/ML Engineer
basics.summary: Software engineer building production AI agents, backend systems, retrieval pipelines, and reliable language-model evaluations.

work.name: Medical Informatics Engineering
work.summary: Building production software and AI-enabled developer tools for healthcare quality measurement.
highlights: Led development of WorkWell Measure Studio, migrating the backend from Java 21/Spring Boot to modular TypeScript while preserving REST contracts and deterministic CQL behavior.
Implemented HL7 FHIR R4, SMART Backend Services, FHIR MeasureReport, MAT-compatible, and QRDA interoperability, validating CMS122 and CMS125 against 121/121 official MADiE test cases.
Scaled audited batch evaluation to 5,000 subjects and 72,100 outcomes with bounded-memory, resumable pipelines and per-subject failure isolation.
Added guardrailed AI and read-only MCP tools for CQL drafting, test-fixture generation, evidence explanation, and agent access while keeping deterministic CQL as the compliance authority.

work.name: Purdue University
work.position: Graduate Researcher - AI Safety & LLM Evaluation
work.summary: Designed reproducible LLM safety and evaluation pipelines for PEFT, jailbreak robustness, and shared-task research.
highlights: Automated FSDP and SLURM pipelines to evaluate 25 LLM configurations across five model families using full fine-tuning, LoRA, QLoRA, FP16, and 4-bit quantization on A100/A30 GPUs.
Executed PAIR, DeepInception, ArtPrompt, and AutoDAN across HarmBench and JailbreakBench, producing 600 attack-success-rate measurements across thousands of completions.
Calibrated three automated safety evaluators against human annotations and applied bootstrap confidence intervals, nonparametric tests, and interaction analysis.
Co-authored two SemEval 2026 system papers on multi-turn RAG and political-response clarity/evasion classification.

work.name: Medical Informatics Engineering
work.summary: Built a production-oriented multi-service RAG platform for semantic, citation-backed search over enterprise documents.
highlights: Architected a containerized RAG platform using Node.js, Docker, OpenSearch, Redis, and PostgreSQL, decoupling asynchronous ingestion from query serving for up to 25 concurrent users and AI agents.

Built a retrieval pipeline combining HyDE query expansion, hybrid search, parent-child retrieval, deduplication, reranking, and feedback boosting; indexed 3,000+ Redmine issues and achieved more than 85% context relevance with TruLens.
Implemented REST, MCP, and SSE interfaces with structured citations, JWT/API-key authentication, user-scoped security filters, and OpenTelemetry observability.

courses: Master's thesis: Do Efficient Adaptations Reduce Safety? Jailbreak Robustness of PEFT vs. Full Fine-Tuning on Consumer-Accessible LLMs

publications.name: SemEval-2026 Task 6: Multi-Seed DeBERTa Ensembles for Political Response Clarity and Evasion Classification
publications.summary: Co-authored system paper for CLARITY; our system ranked 18/41 on clarity and 12/33 on evasion.

publications.name: SemEval-2026 Task 8: Lightweight Tri-Fusion Retrieval with Prompt-Engineered Faithful Generation for Multi-Turn RAG
publications.summary: Co-authored MTRAGEval system paper; our system ranked 20/38 on Task A, 6/26 on Task B, and 14/29 on Task C.

publications.name: Do Efficient Adaptations Reduce Safety? Jailbreak Robustness of PEFT vs. Full Fine-Tuning on Consumer-Accessible LLMs
publications.summary: Master's thesis on fine-tuning methods, jailbreak robustness, evaluator disagreement, and safety-efficiency trade-offs in consumer-accessible LLMs.

projects.name: JobOps Copilot
projects.summary: Deployed human-in-the-loop AI job-operations platform with LangChain agents, RAG, tool calling, and Azure services.

projects.name: WorkWell Measure Studio
projects.summary: Production Total Worker Health compliance platform combining measure authoring, deterministic CQL evaluation, case management, audit trails, exports, and guarded AI assistance.

projects.name: TurboQuant
projects.summary: PyTorch implementation and evaluation of KV-cache compression for long-context LLM inference.

projects.name: RASS / CoRAG
projects.summary: Multi-service retrieval platform with asynchronous ingestion, hybrid search, grounded streaming answers, citations, and observability.

projects.name: UNLP 2026 Ukrainian Document QA
projects.summary: Retrieval-augmented question answering pipeline combining BGE-M3 retrieval, Qwen3 reranking, and Gemma-based multiple-choice answering.
